content: We can frame Self-Refine as a single-perspective iterative polishing baseline, and integrate multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to build a multi-perspective Self-Refine framework that avoids getting stuck in local optimization traps caused by single-perspective self-feedback. This hybrid framework retains the core advantage of original Self-Refine that it requires no additional training or supervision data, while fixing its common flaw of repeated unrecognized errors from consistent self-feedback.
